revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tanao_Gokoku_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社 |名称 = 棚尾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[碧南市]]弥生町 |祭神 = 英霊195柱 |創建 = 不明 |例祭 = 5月第4日曜日 }}
'''棚尾神社'''（たなおじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[碧南市]]弥生町にある[[神社]]。いわゆる[[護国神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 「棚尾招魂社」として創建。1931年（昭和6年）に「忠魂社」に改称。1950年（昭和25年）に「棚尾神社」に改称した<ref name="aichi_e_1">愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）愛知県護国神社 ISBN4-87161-056-X C3014 碧南市民図書館蔵</ref>。祭神は、棚尾地区出身の195柱の英霊である<ref name="aichi_e_1"/>。
<gallery widths="170px"> Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|忠魂碑 </gallery>
== 祭事 == * 例祭：5月第4日曜日
== 周辺 == * 八柱神社 * [[DCMカーマ]] 碧南店
== 出典 == <references/>
{{デフォルトソート:たなおしんしや}} [[Category:碧南市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 棚尾神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[碧南市]]弥生町 |祭神 = 英霊195柱 |創建 = 不明 |例祭 = 5月第4日曜日 }}
'''棚尾神社'''（たなおじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[碧南市]]弥生町にある[[神社]]。いわゆる[[護国神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 「棚尾招魂社」として創建。1931年（昭和6年）に「忠魂社」に改称。1950年（昭和25年）に「棚尾神社」に改称した<ref name="aichi_e_1">愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）愛知県護国神社 ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 碧南市民図書館蔵</ref>。祭神は、棚尾地区出身の195柱の英霊である<ref name="aichi_e_1"/>。
<gallery widths="170px"> Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|忠魂碑 </gallery>
== 祭事 == * 例祭：5月第4日曜日
== 周辺 == * 八柱神社 * [[DCMカーマ]] 碧南店
== 出典 == <references/>
{{デフォルトソート:たなおしんしや}} [[Category:碧南市の歴史]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tanabio Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Tanabio Shrine|ja|棚尾神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] {{Shrine Infobox | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes. The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * Annual Festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * Annual Festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * Annual Festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Gokoku shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted link categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * Annual Festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (企業)}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:棚尾神社]] == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tanao Shrine}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|DCM {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)|12=draft}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)|12=draft|12=draft}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline}} * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tanao Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hekinan]] [[Category:Hekinan history]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Tanao_Gokoku_Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:棚尾神社]] [[sn:anao Gokoku Shrine]]

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hekinan|ja|碧南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area.
