revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:碧南市の歴史
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | image = [[File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|qid=Q495802}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q64226302]] Wikidata name: Tanao-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tanao Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|qid=Q495802}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|qq=|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/DCM (corporate)|qq=|1=DCM|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119658858}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, [[:en:Hekinan|Hekinan]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku shrine]], dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Is this even a Gokoku shrine I am not sure[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 11:28, 12 December 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku shrine}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. Tanao-jinja-monument.jpg|Memorial Monument == Festivals == * festival: Fourth Sunday of May == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/DCM (corporate)|1=DCM|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119658858}} Hekinan Store == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. It is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tanao-jinja]][[ja:棚尾神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tanao-jinja]][[ja:棚尾神社]] [[ja:棚尾神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Tanao-jinja-with-torii.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Tanao Shrine | location = {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan|1=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan | deities = 195 enshrined war heroes | established = Unknown | festival = Fourth Sunday of May | prefecture= {{ill|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|1=Hekinan}} }} '''Tanao Shrine''' ({{nihongo|棚尾神社|1=Tanao Gokoku Shrine|Tanao Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yayoi Town, Hekinan|ja|弥生町 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi Town, Hekinan|1=Yayoi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66041243}}, {{ill|Hekinan|en|Hekinan|WD=Q495802|lt=Hekinan|1=Hekinan}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}, dedicated to the spirits of war heroes.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} The shrine is an external precinct of the nearby {{ill|Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|ja|八柱神社 (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}}. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == History == Tanao Shrine was originally established as Tanao Shōkonsha (招魂社). In 1931, it was renamed Tanao Chūkonsha (忠魂社), and in 1950, it received its current name, Tanao Shrine.『愛知県下英霊社忠魂碑等調査報告書（第一輯）』愛知県護国神社　ISBN 4-87161-056-X C3014 The shrine enshrines the spirits of 195 war heroes from the Tanao area. (碧南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine (Hekinan)|qq=}} * {{ill|DCM (corporate)|ja|DCM (企業)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/DCM (corporate)|1=DCM|comment=changed title based on
